info.title: Devbox API
info.description:
Manage Devbox development environments — create, configure, control lifecycle, and monitor container-based isolated dev environments.

## Authentication

All endpoints require authentication via URL-encoded kubeconfig. Set the `Authorization` header to `encodeURIComponent(kubeconfigYaml)` before sending the request. Obtain your kubeconfig from the Sealos console.

## Errors

All error responses use a unified format:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "type": "validation_error",
    "code": "INVALID_PARAMETER",
    "message": "...",
    "details": [...]
  }
}
```

- `type` — high-level category (e.g. `validation_error`, `resource_error`, `internal_error`)
- `code` — stable identifier for programmatic handling
- `message` — human-readable explanation
- `details` — optional extra context; shape varies by `code`

## Operations

**Query** (read-only): returns `200 OK` with data in the response body.

**Mutation** (write):
- Create (sync) → `201 Created` with the created resource in the response body.
- Create (async) → `202 Accepted` with `{ "name": "...", "status": "creating" }`. Poll the corresponding `GET` endpoint to track progress.
- Update / Delete / Action → `204 No Content` with no response body.

tags.name: Query
tags.description: Read-only operations. Success: `200 OK` with data in the response body.

tags.name: Mutation
tags.description: Write operations. Sync create: `201 Created` with the new resource. Async create: `202 Accepted` with `{ name, status }` (poll GET to track progress). Update/Delete/Action: `204 No Content`.

baseUrl.description: Base URL of your instance (e.g. https://devbox.192.168.x.x.nip.io)

get.description: Retrieve all Devbox instances in the current namespace with resource and runtime information.

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

500.description: Failed to retrieve the devbox list.

error.message: Failed to list devboxes from Kubernetes.

post.description: Create a new Devbox instance with the specified runtime, resources, and network ports. CPU and memory quota must be in the range [0.1, 32].

requestBody.description:
Devbox creation parameters.

**Example — Python devbox with a public HTTP port:**
```json
{
  "name": "my-python-api",
  "runtime": "python",
  "quota": { "cpu": 1, "memory": 2 },
  "ports": [{ "number": 8080, "protocol": "http", "isPublic": true }],
  "env": [],
  "autostart": false
}
```

**Example — Go devbox with environment variables and autostart:**
```json
{
  "name": "my-go-service",
  "runtime": "go",
  "quota": { "cpu": 0.5, "memory": 1 },
  "ports": [],
  "env": [{ "name": "GO_ENV", "value": "development" }],
  "autostart": true
}
```

**Example — minimal resources (floor values):**
```json
{
  "name": "my-minimal-devbox",
  "runtime": "node.js",
  "quota": { "cpu": 0.1, "memory": 0.1 },
  "ports": [],
  "env": [],
  "autostart": false
}
```

name.description: Devbox name (must be DNS compliant: lowercase, numbers, hyphens, 1-63 chars)

quota.description: Resource allocation for CPU and memory

isPublic.description: Enable public domain access, defaults to true

valueFrom.description: Source for the environment variable value

autostart.description: Auto start devbox after creation (defaults to false)

201.description: Devbox created. SSH connection details (port, private key, domain) are provisioned synchronously and returned in this response. The container pod starts asynchronously — poll `GET /devbox/{name}` until `status` is `running` before establishing an SSH connection.

sshPort.description: SSH node port for connecting to the Devbox

base64PrivateKey.description: Base64-encoded SSH private key. Decode before use.

workingDir.description: Default working directory inside the Devbox

domain.description: Base domain of the Sealos instance

error.description: Error message if port creation failed

autostarted.description: Whether the autostart job was triggered (`autostart: true` in request)

portErrors.description: Ports that failed to be created (only present when partial failure occurred)

schema.description: Devbox creation response. The pod is starting asynchronously — poll GET /devbox/{name} until `status` is `running` before establishing an SSH connection.

details.message: String must contain at least 1 character(s)

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

404.description: The specified runtime does not exist or is not available.

error.message: Runtime 'invalid-runtime' not found or not available.

409.description: A Devbox with the specified name already exists.

get.description: Retrieve complete configuration, runtime status, SSH connection information, ports, and pod list for a specific Devbox.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Devbox name (format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, 1–63 characters)

upTime.description: Running duration since first pod started (human-readable)

port.description: SSH port number, null if not yet assigned

value.description: Direct value of the environment variable

schema.title: Get DevBox Detail Response
schema.description: Response schema for getting Devbox details

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

404.description: The specified Devbox does not exist.

patch.description:
Update Devbox resource quota and/or port configuration. CPU and memory quota must be in the range [0.1, 32].

Key points:
- At least one of `quota` or `ports` must be provided.
- To update an existing port supply its `portName`. To add a new port omit `portName`.
- Ports not present in the `ports` array are deleted.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Devbox name (format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, 1–63 characters)

quota.description: Resource allocation for CPU and memory (optional)

portName.description: Existing port name to update (include to update specific port)

isPublic.description: Enable public domain access - optional for updates

number.description: Port number (1-65535) - required for new ports

isPublic.description: Enable public domain access, defaults to true

items.description: Port configuration - include portName to update existing port, omit to create new port

ports.description: Array of port configurations. Include portName to update existing ports, exclude portName to create new ports. Existing ports not included will be deleted. (optional)

schema.title: Update DevBox Request
schema.description: Request schema for updating DevBox resource and/or port configurations

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

404.description: Devbox not found, or a referenced `portName` does not exist.

409.description: Port number is already in use by another port.

422.description: Resource specification rejected by the Kubernetes cluster.

error.details: admission webhook "devbox.sealos.io" denied the request: quota exceeded

delete.description:
Delete a Devbox and all associated resources (services, ingress rules, certificates, persistent volumes).

Key points:
- **Idempotent** — if the Devbox does not exist the request still returns `204`.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Devbox name (format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, 1–63 characters)

204.description: Devbox deleted successfully, or did not exist (idempotent).

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

500.description: Failed to delete the Devbox or its associated resources.

post.description: Configure the command that runs automatically when the Devbox starts. If `execCommand` is omitted the default template entrypoint is used.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Devbox name (format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, 1–63 characters)

requestBody.description:
Autostart configuration. The body is optional — send `{}` to use the default entrypoint.

**Example — custom startup script:**
```json
{
  "execCommand": "/bin/bash /home/devbox/project/startup.sh"
}
```

execCommand.description: Custom command to execute in the devbox (optional)

schema.description: Request body for autostart configuration (optional, can be empty)

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

404.description: The specified Devbox does not exist.

post.description:
Start a paused or stopped Devbox and restore its network ingress rules to active state.

Key points:
- **Idempotent** — calling start on an already-running Devbox returns `204`.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Devbox name (format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, 1–63 characters)

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

404.description: The specified Devbox does not exist.

post.description:
Pause a Devbox to stop its compute resources while preserving port allocations, reducing costs.

Key points:
- **Idempotent** — calling pause on an already-paused Devbox returns `204`.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Devbox name (format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, 1–63 characters)

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

404.description: The specified Devbox does not exist.

post.description: Completely shut down a Devbox, releasing all compute resources and port allocations to minimise costs.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Devbox name (format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, 1–63 characters)

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

404.description: The specified Devbox does not exist.

500.description: Failed to shut down the Devbox.

post.description:
Trigger a complete restart cycle: stop all pods, wait for termination, restore ingress, then start the Devbox.

Key points:
- **Idempotent** — always triggers a restart regardless of the current state.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Devbox name (format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, 1–63 characters)

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

404.description: The specified Devbox does not exist.

error.message: Max retries reached while waiting for devbox pod to be deleted.

get.description: Retrieve all release versions for a Devbox, ordered by creation time descending.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Devbox name (format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, 1–63 characters)

createdAt.description: Creation time in YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm format

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

404.description: The specified Devbox does not exist.

500.description: Failed to retrieve the release list.

post.description:
Snapshot the current Devbox state and trigger a container image build for the given version tag.

Key points:
- **Asynchronous** — returns `202 Accepted` immediately. The build pipeline (stop devbox → build image → restart devbox) runs in the background; poll `GET /devbox/{name}/releases` to track progress.
- By default the Devbox is restarted after the build succeeds (`startDevboxAfterRelease: true`). Set to `false` to keep the Devbox stopped after the release.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Devbox name (format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, 1–63 characters)

requestBody.description:
Release parameters.

**Example — minimal (restart after build, no custom startup command):**
```json
{
  "tag": "v1-2-0",
  "releaseDescription": "Added API improvements and bug fixes."
}
```

**Example — keep Devbox stopped after release:**
```json
{
  "tag": "v1-0-0",
  "releaseDescription": "Hotfix",
  "startDevboxAfterRelease": false
}
```

**Example — restart with autostart command:**
```json
{
  "tag": "v1-3-0",
  "releaseDescription": "Adds startup script",
  "execCommand": "nohup /home/devbox/project/entrypoint.sh > /dev/null 2>&1 &"
}
```

execCommand.description: Command to execute in the devbox after release restart (autostart)

startDevboxAfterRelease.description: Restart devbox automatically after the release build completes. Defaults to `true`.

202.description: Release accepted. The container image build pipeline has started in the background. Poll `GET /devbox/{name}/releases` to track progress.

status.description: Always `creating` — the build is running asynchronously.

details.message: Tag must comply with DNS naming conventions.

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

404.description: The specified Devbox does not exist.

409.description: A release with this tag already exists for this Devbox.

get.summary: List deployed applications from a devbox
get.description: Retrieve all AppLaunchpad applications that were deployed from this Devbox's releases.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Devbox name (format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, 1–63 characters)

tag.description: Devbox tag extracted from image name

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

404.description: The specified Devbox does not exist.

delete.description:
Delete a specific release version and its associated container image.

Key points:
- **Idempotent** — if the release does not exist the request still returns `204`.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Devbox name (format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, 1–63 characters)

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Release version tag (format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, 1–63 characters)

204.description: Release deleted successfully, or did not exist (idempotent).

error.message: Invalid devbox name or release tag format.

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

post.description:
Deploy a successfully built release version as a production application in AppLaunchpad.

Key points:
- The release must be in `Success` status before deploying.
- Each call creates a new AppLaunchpad application; prior deployments are not replaced.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Devbox name (format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, 1–63 characters)

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Release version tag (format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, 1–63 characters)

204.description: Release deployed successfully. Application is now running in AppLaunchpad.

error.message: Invalid devbox name or release tag format.

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

404.description: Devbox or release tag not found, or release is not in Success status.

notFound.summary: Release not found or not successful

error.message: Devbox release tag v1-0-0 is not found or not successful.

get.description: Retrieve time-series CPU and memory usage metrics for a specific Devbox.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Devbox name (format: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens, 1–63 characters)

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the monitoring window. Accepts a Unix timestamp in either **seconds** or **milliseconds** (values > 10¹² are automatically divided by 1000). Defaults to `end − 3 h`.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the monitoring window. Accepts a Unix timestamp in either **seconds** or **milliseconds** (values > 10¹² are automatically divided by 1000). Defaults to the current server time.

schema.description: Chronological sequence of devbox CPU and memory usage

400.description: Invalid devbox name or query parameters.

invalidTimeRange.summary: start is not earlier than end

error.message: Start timestamp must be earlier than end timestamp.

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

error.message: Failed to fetch devbox monitor data.

get.description: Retrieve available runtime environments and their default port/command configurations for creating Devboxes.

runtime.description: Runtime name (from iconId or repository uid)

401.description: No valid credentials provided, or credentials have expired.

500.description: Failed to retrieve the template list.

error.message: Failed to query template repositories from database.

kubeconfigAuth.name: Authorization
kubeconfigAuth.description: URL-encoded kubeconfig YAML. Encode with `encodeURIComponent(kubeconfigYaml)` before setting the header value. Obtain your kubeconfig from the Sealos console.
